# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder_14b)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:2.7-slim

# Install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    build-essential \
    libssl-dev \
    libffi-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set the working directory
WORKDIR /app

# Copy the requirements file
COPY requirements.txt /app/

# Install Python dependencies
RUN pip install --upgrade pip && \
    pip install -r requirements.txt

# Copy the application code
COPY . /app/

# Expose the application port
EXPOSE 8000

# Run the application
CMD ["python", "your_application.py"]
